sections.paragraph: Eze 28:13, a jasper, a precious stone of different colours. (Arab. يَشْفُ, يَشْمُ, يَشَبُ, also يَفْبُ, يَصْبفُ id.) If an etymology is to be sought in Hebrew, it may be from the root שָׁפָה to be smooth [but see יָשַׁף above]. But the form of the word appears strange, as if it were foreign.
